title: Job Training Formats - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Employment > Job Training Formats 25 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions African American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ARC OF BENTON COUNTY ADVOCACY AND DAY SUPPORTS ACTIVITIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy services, an employment training program, and supported recreational activities such as art instruction, poetry classes, bowling, game nights, movies, and others. A community calendar with listings of activities can be found on the website. If one-on-one assistance is required for recreational activities, individuals must provide their own staff. 928 NW Beca Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Recreational activites: Ages 18 and older with intellectual or developmental disabilites and their families All other services: Individuals with intellectual or developmental disabilities and their families. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 602-9225 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONSTRUCTING HOPE PRE-APPRENTICESHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 10 weeks of construction training including classroom instruction, hands-on experience, certifications, and site visits to apprenticeship training centers. 405 NE Church Street Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following qualifications: -Age 18 and older -US citizen or legal resident -High school diploma or GED with reading and math skills at an 8th-grade level -Must have or be able to get a state driver’s license and auto insurance -Pass drug screening Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-noon and 1pm-4pm, Friday by appointment only Quarterly sessions each year begin in January, April, June, and September Email (503) 281-1234 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RIVERFRONT SCHOOL AND CAREER CENTER RIVER FRONT SCHOOL AND CAREER CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accredited alternative school offering educational and vocational training. Services include comprehensive career and basic skill assessment, computer skills instruction, work readiness and job search assistance, and job training programs. Assists with transportation, job search clothing, child care, and GED testing. 1666 W 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: At-risk and out-of-school youth ages 14-21 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (541) 302-2554 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION OREGON HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION OF JACKSON AND JOSEPHINE COUNTY NATIONAL FARMWORKER JOBS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment and training services to assist farmworkers (seasonal or migrant) in obtaining permanent and higher-paying employment opportunities. English as a second language, GED/High school equivalency test instruction, career services, classes, training, and on-the-job experiences, skills training and certifications for farmworkers, support with the job application and search process, career counseling, financial assistance for college, and education and career services for agricultural workers and their dependents. Depending on location and funding availability, specific classes, training opportunities, and financial assistance will vary. 820 S Central Avenue Suite A Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are an agricultural worker, the dependent, or spouse of an agricultural worker who meet federal income guidelines and are authorized to in the United States. Must be enrolled or in the process of enrolling at an accredited community college, vocational school/program, apprenticeship, or 4-year university. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm (541) 841-2551 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION OREGON HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION REGION 21 NATIONAL FARMWORKER JOBS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment and training services to assist farmworkers (seasonal or migrant) in obtaining permanent and higher-paying employment opportunities. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 881-1491 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE BUREAU OF LABOR AND INDUSTRIES APPRENTICESHIP AND TRAINING DIVISION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Regulates apprenticeships in various jobs, partnering with businesses, labor groups, and schools to create more training and job opportunities. Apprenticeships mix hands-on work with classroom learning, and employers run the programs while paying apprentices. The division ensures these programs offer quality training and equal chances, especially for women and minorities. Some apprenticeship programs may provide financial assistance for child care. 1800 SW 1st Avenue Suite 500 Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (971) 245-3844 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES SELF SUFFICIENCY ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE SNAP TRAINING AND EMPLOYMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists SNAP recipients to gain skills, training, work experience, and employment to increase their ability to obtain living-wage employment. May be able to cover the cost of training, textbooks, gas, bus tickets, housing and utility costs, and work clothes. 500 Summer Street NE E48 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: The STEP program is for people in Oregon who: -Are receiving SNAP benefits, -Are at least 16 years old, and -Are not receiving TANF benefits. The ABAWD program is for people in Oregon who: -Ages 18-49 -No children under 18 years old on their SNAP case -Do not meet an exemption or live in an exempt county Hours: Monday- Friday 8am-5pm (833) 947-1964 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON TRADESWOMEN TRADES AND APPRENTICESHIP CAREER CLASS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a 7-week, state-certified, pre-apprenticeship training class in the skilled construction trades that provides students with the following: -Learn basic trades, math, and measurement -Learn to use hand and power tools -Receive an introduction to green building -Explore topics such as job site safety and construction culture -Gain 30 hours of hands-on experience working alongside skilled female instructors on actual job sites -Field trips to apprenticeship training centers and active construction sites -Improve physical fitness with strength training taught by a certified fitness trainer Career counselors assist program graduates with job searches and application processes to apprenticeship training programs upon graduation. Career counselors also provide individualized employment counseling throughout the entire course of the graduate's trades career. 16790 NE Mason Street Suite 100 Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: -Women and other gender minorities -Age 18 years or older -GED or high school diploma -Ability to pass a drug test -Attend an information session -Take the National Career Readiness Certificate (NCRC) test Additional enrollment requirements may be required and are available during the initial informational session Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (503) 335-8200 121 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE OPPORTUNITY CENTER AT 42ND AVENUE OPPORTUNITY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides career exploration focusing on immigrant, refugee, and non-native English speakers. Offers skills-based training, workshops, and noncredit classes on healthcare, manufacturing, technology, and construction trades. Courses on financial literacy, basic computer workshops, and the Steps to Success program for Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) are available. 4299 NE Killingsworth Street Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Varies by program or class Email (971) 722-2000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE WILLOW CREEK OPPORTUNITY CENTER OPPORTUNITY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides career exploration focusing on immigrant, refugee, and non-native English speakers. Offers skills-based training, workshops, and noncredit classes on healthcare, manufacturing, technology, and construction trades. Courses on financial literacy, basic computer workshops, and the Steps to Success program for Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) are available. 241 SW Edgeway Drive Beaverton, OR 97006 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Varies by program or class Email (971) 722-2688 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND YOUTHBUILDERS BRIDGE TO CONSTRUCTION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a 9-week construction training program for high school graduates pursuing a trade career. Participants gain hands-on job experience while working on active construction sites. Training includes power- and hand-tool training, professional certifications, and career counseling. Participants receive a modest living stipend while attending the program. Provides a pre-apprenticeship program giving students direct-entry status to many local construction apprenticeship programs. 4816 SE 92nd Avenue Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Ages 18-26 with a high school diploma or GED. Applicants must be highly motivated, attend full-time, and pass frequent drug tests to continue in the program. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 286-9350 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RELAY RESOURCES COMPETITIVE INTEGRATED EMPLOYMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides personalized career counseling, employment training, and employment guidance to individuals who experience barriers to employment. 5312 NE 148th Avenue Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Adults with disabilities and neurodivergent individuals. Includes those with physical limitations, learning disabilities, intellectual and/or developmental disabilities, and mental health conditions. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 261-1266 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHERN OREGON GOODWILL SOUTHERN OREGON GOODWILL JACKSON COUNTY WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT AND JOB CONNECTION GOODWORKS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers and on-the-job training program including life skills, computer literacy skills, and internsips. 11 W Jackson Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4pm Email (541) 772-3300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHWESTERN OREGON WORKFORCE INVESTMENT BOARD WORKSOURCE COOS APPRENTICESHIP PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment and training services, career counseling, and skill upgrade courses. Also provides apprentiiceship and internship opportunities in the Early Childhood Education (CCE), Certified Clinical Medical Assistant (CCMA), and Surgical Technologist fields. 990 S 2nd Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (844) 532-6893 9 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHWESTERN OREGON WORKFORCE INVESTMENT BOARD WORKSOURCE CURRY APPRENTICESHIP PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment and training services, career counseling, and skill upgrade courses. Also provides apprentiiceship and internship opportunities in the Early Childhood Education (CCE), Certified Clinical Medical Assistant (CCMA), and Surgical Technologist fields. 16261 Highway 101 S Suite 11 Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (844) 532-6893 9 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHWESTERN OREGON WORKFORCE INVESTMENT BOARD WORKSOURCE DOUGLAS APPRENTICESHIP PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment and training services, career counseling, and skill upgrade courses. Also provides apprentiiceship and internship opportunities in the Early Childhood Education (CCE), Certified Clinical Medical Assistant (CCMA), and Surgical Technologist fields. 846 SE Pine Street Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (844) 532-6893 9 Get Directions Visit Website More Details STONE SOUP STONE SOUP NE CULINARY JOB TRAINING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 12-week training program to assist individuals who want to work in the the food and hospitality industries. The program gives each participant hands-on training to prepare them for employment. Program participants receive a stipend. This program operates on a waitlist. 7400 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following requirements: -Age 18 and older with a focus on those with housing insecurity and barriers to employment -Currently housed. Individuals currently in shelters or transitional housing are considered as being housed for purposes of this program. -Possess basic reading and writing comprehension in English language -Must not be a registered sex offender Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 719-4772 Get Directions Visit Website More Details STONE SOUP STONE SOUP SE CULINARY JOB TRAINING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 12-week training program to assist individuals who want to work in the the food and hospitality industries. The program gives each participant hands-on training to prepare them for employment. Program participants receive a stipend. This program operates on a waitlist. 2740 SE Powell Boulevard Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following requirements: -Age 18 and older with a focus on those with housing insecurity and barriers to employment -Currently housed. Individuals currently in shelters or transitional housing are considered as being housed for purposes of this program. -Possess basic reading and writing comprehension in English language -Must not be a registered sex offender Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 719-4772 Get Directions Visit Website More Details URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND YOUTH PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers youth programs with interactive academic and real-world experiences that build leadership, life, and social skills, self-esteem, and cultural pride. Programs are designed to prepare students for success, encouraging post-secondary education and professional careers. Services include scholarship listings, job readiness training, employment support, and internship opportunities. 3934 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Ages 12-25. Focuses on serving the African American community and additional communities of color.
